quizQuestion:
Si tu avais étudié, tu {___} l'examen.
(If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé
explanation: aurais réussi — conditionnel passé of réussir. The consequence clause uses avoir in conditionnel présent (aurais) + past participle réussi. The si-clause uses plus-que-parfait.

quizQuestion:
Si j'avais su, je {___}.
(If I had known, I would have come.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had known, I would have come.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — venir (être verb) in conditionnel passé
explanation: serais venu — conditionnel passé of venir (être verb). Je serais venu(e): être in conditionnel présent (serais) + past participle venu, agreeing with subject gender.

quizQuestion:
Si elle était partie plus tôt, elle {___} le train.
(If she had left earlier, she would have caught the train.)
sentenceTranslation: If she had left earlier, she would have caught the train.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — prendre in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait pris — conditionnel passé of prendre (irregular past participle pris). Elle aurait pris: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + pris.

quizQuestion:
Je serais venu si j'{___}.
(I would have come if I had known.)
sentenceTranslation: I would have come if I had known.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — consequence clause first, si-clause second
explanation: avais su — plus-que-parfait of savoir. J'avais su: avoir in imparfait (avais) + past participle su. Either clause may come first; when the si-clause follows, no comma is used.

quizQuestion:
Si nous avions fait attention, nous {___} cette erreur.
(If we had paid attention, we would have avoided this mistake.)
sentenceTranslation: If we had paid attention, we would have avoided this mistake.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — éviter in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurions évité — conditionnel passé of éviter. Nous aurions évité: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurions) + past participle évité.

quizQuestion:
S'il avait travaillé plus dur, il {___} le match.
(If he had worked harder, he would have won the match.)
sentenceTranslation: If he had worked harder, he would have won the match.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — gagner in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait gagné — conditionnel passé of gagner. Il aurait gagné: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + past participle gagné.

quizQuestion:
Si vous aviez appelé, ils {___} vous aider.
(If you had called, they would have been able to help you.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had called, they would have been able to help you.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — pouvoir in conditionnel passé
explanation: auraient pu — conditionnel passé of pouvoir (irregular past participle pu). Ils auraient pu + infinitive: unrealized possibility in the past.

quizQuestion:
Si le bus était parti à l'heure, ils {___} à temps.
(If the bus had left on time, they would have arrived on time.)
sentenceTranslation: If the bus had left on time, they would have arrived on time.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — arriver (être verb) in conditionnel passé
explanation: seraient arrivés — conditionnel passé of arriver (être verb). Ils seraient arrivés: être in conditionnel présent (seraient) + past participle arrivés, agreeing with plural ils.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} la vérité si tu le lui avais demandé.
(She would have told the truth if you had asked her.)
sentenceTranslation: She would have told the truth if you had asked her.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — dire in conditionnel passé, consequence first
explanation: aurait dit — conditionnel passé of dire (irregular past participle dit). Elle aurait dit: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + dit. Consequence clause comes first here.

quizQuestion:
Si nous avions su le prix, nous {___} autant.
(If we had known the price, we would not have spent so much.)
sentenceTranslation: If we had known the price, we would not have spent so much.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — negated conditionnel passé
explanation: n'aurions pas dépensé — negated conditionnel passé of dépenser. Negation wraps the auxiliary: nous n'aurions pas + past participle dépensé.

quizQuestion:
Si j'avais eu le temps, j'{___} cette exposition.
(If I had had the time, I would have seen this exhibition.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had had the time, I would have seen this exhibition.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — voir in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurais vu — conditionnel passé of voir (irregular past participle vu). J'aurais vu: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurais) + vu.

quizQuestion:
Si on avait prévu ce problème, il {___} agir plus vite.
(If this problem had been foreseen, it would have been necessary to act faster.)
sentenceTranslation: If this problem had been foreseen, it would have been necessary to act faster.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — falloir (impersonal) in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait fallu — conditionnel passé of falloir (impersonal). Il aurait fallu + infinitive. Falloir has irregular past participle fallu; only the il form exists.

quizQuestion:
Il aurait réussi s'il {___} davantage.
(He would have succeeded if he had studied more.)
sentenceTranslation: He would have succeeded if he had studied more.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — identifying the plus-que-parfait in si-clause
explanation: avait étudié — plus-que-parfait of étudier. S'il avait étudié: avoir in imparfait (avait) + past participle étudié. The consequence clause comes first here.

quizQuestion:
Si on lui avait donné le choix, elle {___} autrement.
(If she had been given the choice, she would have chosen differently.)
sentenceTranslation: If she had been given the choice, she would have chosen differently.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — choisir in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait choisi — conditionnel passé of choisir. Elle aurait choisi: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + past participle choisi.

quizQuestion:
Si tu avais accepté leur offre, ta vie {___} différente.
(If you had accepted their offer, your life would have been different.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had accepted their offer, your life would have been different.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — être in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait été — conditionnel passé of être (irregular past participle été). Ta vie aurait été: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + été.

quizQuestion:
Si j'avais étudié la médecine, je {___} maintenant.
(If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past si-clause → present consequence
explanation: serais médecin — conditionnel présent of être. The si-clause uses plus-que-parfait (past hypothesis), but the consequence uses conditionnel présent because the result is a present state (being a doctor now). This is the defining feature of the mixed conditional.

quizQuestion:
Si tu n'avais pas raté ton avion, tu {___}.
(If you hadn't missed your plane, you would already be in Paris.)
sentenceTranslation: If you hadn't missed your plane, you would already be in Paris.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past si-clause → present location consequence
explanation: serais déjà à Paris — conditionnel présent of être + location. The past event (missing the flight) produces a present consequence (not being in Paris yet). Conditionnel présent, not conditionnel passé, signals the present-tense result.

quizQuestion:
Si tu avais suivi les cours assidûment, tu {___}.
(If you had attended classes regularly, you would speak better French now.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had attended classes regularly, you would speak better French now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past learning → present ability consequence
explanation: parlerais mieux français — conditionnel présent of parler. The past study (hypothetical) produces a present skill level (speaking better now). Conditionnel présent marks this ongoing consequence.

quizQuestion:
S'il avait pris soin de sa santé, il {___} aujourd'hui.
(If he had taken care of his health, he would feel better today.)
sentenceTranslation: If he had taken care of his health, he would feel better today.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past habits → present health state
explanation: se sentirait mieux — conditionnel présent of se sentir (reflexive). Past health habits (hypothetical) → current physical wellbeing. Conditionnel présent, not passé, marks the present-state consequence.

quizQuestion:
Si nous avions économisé, nous {___} maintenant.
(If we had saved money, we would be able to travel now.)
sentenceTranslation: If we had saved money, we would be able to travel now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past saving → present ability with pouvoir
explanation: pourrions voyager — conditionnel présent of pouvoir + infinitive. Past saving (hypothetical) → present ability to travel. Nous pourrions: conditionnel présent marks the current capability.

quizQuestion:
Si elle avait accepté le poste, elle {___} en ce moment.
(If she had accepted the position, she would be working in Lyon right now.)
sentenceTranslation: If she had accepted the position, she would be working in Lyon right now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past decision → present ongoing situation
explanation: travaillerait à Lyon — conditionnel présent of travailler + location. A past decision (accepting the post) produces a present ongoing consequence (working there now). Conditionnel présent signals the present-tense result.

quizQuestion:
Si j'avais mieux dormi hier soir, je {___} maintenant.
(If I had slept better last night, I would be less tired now.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had slept better last night, I would be less tired now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past night's sleep → present energy level
explanation: serais moins fatigué — conditionnel présent of être + adjective. Yesterday's sleep quality (past hypothesis) → today's energy level (present state). Conditionnel présent, not passé, marks the ongoing consequence.

quizQuestion:
Si elle n'avait pas quitté Paris, elle {___}.
(If she hadn't left Paris, she would still be living there.)
sentenceTranslation: If she hadn't left Paris, she would still be living there.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past departure → present ongoing residence
explanation: habiterait encore là-bas — conditionnel présent of habiter. The past departure (hypothetically not occurring) → present ongoing residence. Conditionnel présent marks this current-state consequence.

quizQuestion:
Si tu avais voulu attraper ce bus, tu {___} plus tôt.
(If you had wanted to catch that bus, you would have had to leave earlier.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had wanted to catch that bus, you would have had to leave earlier.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — devoir in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait dû partir — conditionnel passé of devoir + infinitive. Tu aurais dû partir: devoir in conditionnel passé (aurait dû) + infinitive partir. Expresses unrealized obligation in the past.

quizQuestion:
Si j'avais eu plus de temps libre, j'{___} voyager davantage.
(If I had had more free time, I would have liked to travel more.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had had more free time, I would have liked to travel more.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — aimer in conditionnel passé (wish/preference)
explanation: aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer. J'aurais aimé + infinitive expresses an unrealized wish or preference in the past. Pattern sourced from Tatoeba example 12077.

quizQuestion:
Si elle avait grandi bilingue, elle {___} travailler à l'international maintenant.
(If she had grown up bilingual, she would want to work internationally now.)
sentenceTranslation: If she had grown up bilingual, she would want to work internationally now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past upbringing → present desire consequence
explanation: voudrait — conditionnel présent of vouloir. A past formative experience (growing up bilingual) → present desire or inclination. Conditionnel présent marks this current-state want.

quizQuestion:
Si tu lui avais expliqué la situation, il {___} mieux.
(If you had explained the situation to him, he would have understood better.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had explained the situation to him, he would have understood better.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — comprendre in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait compris — conditionnel passé of comprendre (irregular past participle compris). Il aurait compris: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + compris.

quizQuestion:
Si elle avait appris à cuisiner enfant, elle {___} mieux maintenant.
(If she had learned to cook as a child, she would know how to cook better now.)
sentenceTranslation: If she had learned to cook as a child, she would know how to cook better now.
grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — childhood past → present skill with savoir
explanation: saurait cuisiner — conditionnel présent of savoir + infinitive. Past learning (childhood) → present ability or knowledge. Savoir in conditionnel présent marks the current skill state.

quizQuestion:
S'il avait mangé sainement toute sa vie, il {___} plus longtemps.
(If he had eaten healthily all his life, he would have lived longer.)
sentenceTranslation: If he had eaten healthily all his life, he would have lived longer.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — vivre in conditionnel passé
explanation: aurait vécu — conditionnel passé of vivre (irregular past participle vécu). Il aurait vécu: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + vécu.

quizQuestion:
S'ils avaient écouté les conseils, ils {___} cette catastrophe.
(If they had listened to the advice, they would have been able to avoid this disaster.)
sentenceTranslation: If they had listened to the advice, they would have been able to avoid this disaster.
grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — pouvoir + infinitive in conditionnel passé
explanation: auraient pu éviter — conditionnel passé of pouvoir + infinitive. Ils auraient pu éviter: avoir in conditionnel présent (auraient) + pu (irregular) + infinitive éviter. Modal in conditionnel passé expresses unrealized possibility.
